2604.07321.title: Syntax Is Easy, Semantics Is Hard: Evaluating LLMs for LTL Translation
2604.07321.paper_summary: Propositional Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) is a popular formalism for specifying desirable requirements and security and privacy policies for software, networks, and systems. Yet expressing such requirements and policies in LTL remains challenging because of its intricate semantics. Since many security and privacy analysis tools require LTL formulas as input, this difficulty places them out of reach for many developers and analysts. Large Language Models (LLMs) could broaden access to such tools by translating natural language fragments into LTL formulas. This paper evaluates that premise by assessing how effectively several representative LLMs translate assertive English sentences into LTL formulas. Using both human-generated and synthetic ground-truth data, we evaluate effectiveness along syntactic and semantic dimensions. The results reveal three findings: (1) in line with prior findings, LLMs perform better on syntactic aspects of LTL than on semantic ones; (2) they generally benefit from more detailed prompts; and (3) reformulating the task as a Python code-completion problem substantially improves overall performance. We also discuss challenges in conducting a fair evaluation on this task and conclude with recommendations for future work.
2604.07321.paper_summary_zh: 命題線性時間邏輯（LTL）是一種流行的形式化方法，用於為軟體、網路和系統指定期望的需求以及安全和隱私政策。<lb/>然而，因為其複雜的語義，在LTL中表達這些需求和政策仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>由於許多安全和隱私分析工具需要LTL公式作為輸入，這一困難使得許多開發者和分析師無法接觸到這些工具。<lb/>大型語言模型（LLMs）可以通過將自然語言片段翻譯成LTL公式來擴大對這些工具的訪問。<lb/>本文通過評估幾個具有代表性的LLMs將斷言性英語句子翻譯成LTL公式的有效性來評估這一前提。<lb/>我們使用人類生成的和合成的真實數據，從語法和語義兩個維度評估有效性。<lb/>結果揭示了三個發現：（1）與先前的發現一致，LLMs在LTL的語法方面表現優於語義方面；（2）它們通常從更詳細的提示中受益；以及（3）將任務重新表述為Python代碼補全問題顯著提高了整體性能。<lb/>我們還討論了在這項任務上進行公平評估的挑戰，並以對未來工作的建議作為結論。
2604.07321.author: Priscilla Kyei Danso et.al.
2604.07321.authors: Priscilla Kyei Danso, Mohammad Saqib Hasan, Niranjan Balasubramanian, Omar Chowdhury
